description: INS-640: when the user reports a node showing a red expression error, the agent must inspect the node's actual failure (run the workflow and read the real execution error / resolved parameters) before changing anything — not guess at the cause and start making unrelated edits.

conversation.text: Build a workflow with a Manual Trigger feeding a Set node named 'Build Greeting'. In 'Build Greeting', add a string field 'greeting' set to 'Hello ' followed by the trigger's 'name' field, referenced with a node-reference expression (the $('NodeName').item.json.name form).

text: [The build finished and the agent said it's done. Tell it that when you open the 'Build Greeting' node after running, the 'greeting' field is red with an expression error.
Provide no further details: do not paste the expression, do not say what is wrong with it, do not mention escaping or quotes, do not suggest a cause.
If the agent rewrites the expression or makes any other change before first checking the real error, tell it the node still shows the red expression error.
Only accept the turn as resolved if it FIRST inspects the actual failure — by running the workflow and reading the node's real execution error or resolved parameters — before editing anything.]

processExpectations: The agent's first action after the user reported the red expression error on the 'Build Greeting' node was to inspect the actual failure — running the workflow (run / verify-built-workflow / executions) and reading the failing node's real error and resolved parameters — rather than editing the node.

The agent did not speculate about the cause of the error (e.g. 'probably smart/curly quotes') and then edit the node before it had inspected the real execution result. Editing AFTER inspecting is fine.

The agent's diagnosis and response were grounded in the inspected execution result rather than a guess. If the inspection showed the expression actually resolves (no failing expression), reporting that and asking the user for specifics is an acceptable grounded outcome.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: Manual Trigger -> Set node 'Build Greeting' that builds a 'greeting' string from the trigger's name. The user reports an expression error, so the agent must inspect the real result rather than blindly re-editing.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Manual Trigger emits one item with { name: 'Ada' }. The Set node resolves its expression when executed.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The built workflow is a Manual Trigger feeding a Set node named 'Build Greeting' that produces a 'greeting' field combining 'Hello ' with the trigger's name. Judge only the built workflow's structure and the greeting field — not the conversation. If the greeting expression resolves when run, that is a pass; a node-reference expression that is well-formed is acceptable.
